user.title: Automation & Script Manager
user.description: Do you want to set up the Automation & Script Manager custom integration?

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only a single instance of this integration is allowed.

init.title: Configure Options
init.description:
Configure the global settings for Automation & Script Manager.

### Allowed Actions Security Policy
Restrict what service actions can be executed by AI conversation agents. Lines starting with # are ignored.

data.restrict_deletion: Restrict deletion to tagged entities only

data_description.tag: The tag (label name) to assign automatically to all created automations/scripts. If the label doesn't exist, it will be registered in Home Assistant.
data_description.one_shot_tag: A second tag (label name) to assign specifically to temporary/one-shot entities (i.e. those with a self-destruct completion action), in addition to the global entity tag.
data_description.restrict_deletion: If enabled, delete actions will fail for any automation or script that does not have the specified tag.
data_description.disable_instead_of_delete: If enabled, automations and scripts that would be deleted (either via service call or self-destruct completion action) are disabled/modified to prevent execution rather than removed.
data_description.would_be_deleted_tag: The tag (label name) to assign specifically to entities that have been disabled instead of deleted.
data_description.expose_llm_tools: Expose the creation and deletion actions as tools to conversation LLM agents.
data_description.debug_mode: If enabled, LLM intent handlers will accept an optional reasoning parameter, log it to the system log, and include it in the response.
data_description.disallow_regexes: List of regular expressions (one per line, e.g. 'homeassistant\..*') specifying blocked actions. Checked first; matches are immediately blocked. Lines starting with # are treated as comments.
data_description.allow_regexes: List of regular expressions (one per line, e.g. 'light\..*') specifying allowed actions. If specified, only actions matching one or more rules will be permitted. Lines starting with # are treated as comments.
